popularStory.headline: History of Robert Cary (died 1431)
popularStory.description: Sir Robert Cary (died c. 1431) of Cockington, Devon, was twelve times Member of Parliament for Devon, in 1407, 1410, 1411, May 1413, April 1414, Mar. 1416, 1417, 1419, May 1421, 1422, 1425 and 1426. M …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Cary of Cockington於大約1377年出生在英國時，他的父親John Cary29歲，母親Margaret Holway25歲。他於1414年10月8日之前與Jane Hanchford在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1407年被列為member of parliament (12 times during these years)。他去世於大約1431年在他的家鄉享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert Cary of Cockington于大约1377年出生在英国时，他的父亲John Cary29岁，母亲Margaret Holway25岁。他于1414年10月8日之前与Jane Hanchford在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1407年被列为member of parliament (12 times during these years)。他去世于大约1431年在他的家乡享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Donegal and Westmeath) and English: variant of   Carey  .

variants.name: Decorah
meanings: Americanized form of de Carrie, itself an altered form of French Decary, a variant of Cary 2, with fused preposition de ‘of’. It is found mainly among the Ho-Chunk (i.e. Winnebago) Native American people in WI.

History: The progenitor of the Decorahs was a French soldier, Joseph Sabrevoir de Carrie, who in 1729 settled for a time among the Winnebagos on Doty Island, WI, and married an Indian woman, known in English as Glory of the Morning. Decorah, a city in IA, is named after one of their descendants, Waukon Decorah (c. 1780–1868), a chief of the Winnebago.

variants.name: Carey
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ciardha, a midland surname meaning ‘descendant of Ciardha’, a personal name derived from ciar ‘dark, black’. See Kerry .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhiachra ‘son of Fiachra’.

English: from Carey Barton in Devon or Castle Cary, Lytes Cary, Cary Fitzpaine, or Babcary, all in Somerset, named for the rivers on which they stand; both river names probably derive from the Celtic root car- ‘love, liking’, perhaps with the meaning ‘pleasant stream’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Cockington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places called Lockington (in Leicestershire and East Yorkshire). The placename derives from the Old English personal name Loca + the Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Cary of Cockington was born about 1377, in England, his father, John Cary, was 29 and his mother, Margaret Holway, was 25. He married Jane Hanchford before 8 October 1414, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. In 1407, his occupation is listed as member of parliament (12 times during these years). He died about 1431, in his hometown, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Cary of Cockington est né environ 1377, à Angleterre, son père, John Cary, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margaret Holway, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Jane Hanchford avant 8 octobre 1414, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. En 1407, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament (12 times during these years). Il est décédé environ 1431, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert Cary of Cockington um 1377 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Cary 29 und seine Mutter Margaret Holway 25. Er hat vor 8. Oktober 1414 in England Jane Hanchford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1407 ist sein Beruf als member of parliament (12 times during these years) aufgeführt. Er starb um 1431 im Alter von 56 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Cary of Cockington è nato il circa 1377 nel England, il suo padre, John Cary, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Holway, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Jane Hanchford prima del 8 ottobre 1414, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Nel 1407, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament (12 times during these years). Morì circa 1431, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Cary of Cockingtonが約1377年にイギリスで生まれた時，父John Caryは29歳，母Margaret Holwayは25歳であった。1414年10月8日以前にイギリスでJane Hanchfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1407年，職業はmember of parliament (12 times during these years)と記録されている。彼は約1431年に故郷で56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Cary of Cockington(이)가 대략 1377년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Cary(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Holway(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1414년 10월 8일 이전 영국에서 Jane Hanchford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1407년, 그의 직업은 member of parliament (12 times during these years)이었다. 그는 대략 1431년 그의 고향에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Cary of Cockington nasceu aproximadamente 1377, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John Cary, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Holway, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Hanchford antes 8 de outubro de 1414, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Cary of Cockington родился около 1377 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John Cary было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Holway, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Hanchford перед 8 октябрь 1414 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1407 году он работал по профессии member of parliament (12 times during these years). Он умер около 1431 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 56 лет.
